name: Maven MCP Server
what_it_does: MCP server providing Maven build tool capabilities to AI agents. Enables agents to run Maven commands, manage Java project dependencies, query Maven Central repository, check for dependency updates, and interact with Maven build lifecycle — supporting AI-assisted Java development workflows.
use_cases: Running Maven build commands (compile, test, package) from Java development agents
Querying Maven Central for library versions and dependencies from coding agents
Managing pom.xml dependencies and checking for updates from DevOps agents
Integrating Maven build status into AI-driven Java development workflows
not_for: Gradle users (different build tool — use Gradle-specific MCP)
Production build pipelines without proper CI/CD setup
best_when: A Java developer using Maven for project builds wants AI agents to run builds, check dependencies, and interact with the Maven ecosystem — automating routine Java build tasks.
avoid_when: You use Gradle, sbt, or other JVM build tools. Maven is specific to Maven projects.

auth.notes: No authentication for local Maven operations. Maven Central queries use public API. Private repository credentials via Maven settings.xml.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free — Maven is open source, Maven Central is free
pricing.notes: Maven is free open source (Apache). MCP server is free open source.

performance.rate_limits: Local builds — no rate limits; Maven Central API has reasonable rate limits

known_agent_gotchas: Maven build failures can be complex — agents may need to iterate on pom.xml corrections
Maven downloads dependencies on first run — initial build can be slow with large dep trees
Private repository credentials in settings.xml should not be exposed to agents directly
Maven versions (3.x vs wrapper) can affect build behavior — ensure consistent Maven version
agent_readiness.security_notes: Local Maven builds. Maven Central HTTPS. Private repository credentials via settings.xml — keep credentials secure. Build artifacts stay local.

security_score_components.security_notes: Local builds. Maven Central HTTPS. Private repo credentials in settings.xml must be secured.
